prompt: A single page from a 13th-century Gothic illuminated manuscript, rendered on aged cream vellum/parchment. The central composition is an Arbor Scientiae (Tree of Knowledge) diagram that depicts a machine learning Decision Tree classifier. COMPOSITION AND STRUCTURE: The tree grows upward from a thick trunk at the bottom center, rooted in soil with intertwined roots labeled DATASET in Gothic blackletter uncial script. The tree branches symmetrically upward in three levels of binary splits, following the medieval Tree of Porphyry convention. Each internal decision node is rendered as an ornate gilded roundel (circular medallion) with burnished gold leaf background and a thin vermilion border ring. Inside each roundel, the splitting condition is written in Gothic minuscule calligraphy: Age > 30, Income <= 50k, Education = High, Years > 5. Branches connecting the nodes are rendered as organic vine tendrils with small acanthus leaves, with Yes and No labels in red rubric script along each branch. Terminal leaf nodes are rendered as heraldic shield shapes, each painted in a different historically accurate pigment: ultramarine blue for Approved, vermilion red for Denied, verdigris green for Review. BORDER AND MARGINALIA: The entire page is framed by an elaborate rectangular border of interlocking vine scroll (white vine / bianchi girari) patterns in gold, ultramarine, and vermilion. Corner pieces feature gilded quatrefoil medallions. Small drolleries (whimsical marginalia creatures) inhabit the borders: a monk studying a scroll, a small dragon. At the top center of the border, a decorated rubric title reads ARBOR DECISIONIS in red and blue alternating lombardic capitals. COLOR PALETTE (strictly medieval pigments): Deep ultramarine blue (lapis lazuli) for important elements and backgrounds of key nodes. Bright vermilion red for rubrics, borders, and accents. Burnished gold leaf for roundel backgrounds, border elements, and highlights. Verdigris green for foliage and some classifications. Ivory/cream parchment base. Carbon black for outlines and text. Lead white for highlights. Yellow ochre/orpiment for secondary accents. STYLE AND TECHNIQUE: Flat, non-perspectival composition typical of Gothic manuscripts. Precise iron gall ink pen outlines with tempera paint fills. No photographic shading or realistic lighting — only flat color fields with minimal modeling via white highlight lines. Text in authentic Gothic textura quadrata or rotunda letterforms. Visible ruling lines faintly on the parchment. The overall page layout follows the rigid geometric planning of a medieval scriptorium. Visual hierarchy where the root node is largest and terminal nodes are smallest. Small Latin annotations in the margins explaining Gini impurity and Entropia (entropy). The image must look like an authentic folio from a 13th-14th century French or English Gothic manuscript workshop, with the decision tree seamlessly integrated as if it were a medieval scientific diagram like Ramon Llull's Arbor Scientiae. No modern elements, no photographic realism, no digital artifacts.
concept: Decision Tree Classifier — a supervised machine learning algorithm that recursively partitions data via binary feature splits (nodes) into classification outcomes (leaf nodes), using metrics like Gini impurity and entropy
tradition_approach: Targeted burnished gold roundels for nodes, heraldic shields for leaf classifications, vine-scroll borders, lombardic rubric titles, flat non-perspectival composition, and historically accurate pigment palette (ultramarine, vermilion, verdigris, gold leaf) — fusing the decision tree with the Arbor Scientiae diagrammatic tradition.
